summary: Atwood shares a screenshot of a paradoxical Windows dialog box that creates an impossible situation for the user. The dialog apparently requires an action that cannot be completed due to the dialog itself being present. He references the famous quote from WarGames about the only winning move being not to play, suggesting that some software problems are best solved by abandoning the attempt entirely.
key_points: Catch-22 situations in software UI represent fundamental design failures
Sometimes the best solution to a software problem is to avoid engaging with it at all
Poor dialog design can create logically impossible user scenarios
money_quotes: A strange game. The only winning move is not to play. How about a nice game of chess?
key_insight: Some software interfaces are so poorly designed that they create logically impossible situations, leaving users with no choice but to abandon the task entirely.
